questions.question: A graph data structure consists of which two components?
Which data structures are considered special cases of a graph?
In an undirected graph, if edge (A, B) exists, which of the following is also true?
In a directed graph, an arrow from vertex 1 to vertex 2 means:
options: 2 can be visited from 1 and 1 from 2
Only 2 can be visited from 1 directly

questions.question: In a weighted graph, what does the weight on an edge represent?
options: The effort or cost to traverse that edge

questions.question: An unweighted graph can be considered a special case of a weighted graph where every edge weight equals:
The degree of a vertex in an undirected graph is defined as:
options: The number of vertices in the graph
The number of edges connected to that vertex
The shortest path length from the root
The number of cycles through the vertex

questions.question: In a directed graph, the indegree of a vertex is:
options: Number of edges leaving the vertex
Number of edges terminating at the vertex
Total edges connected to the vertex

questions.question: A simple path is one in which:
options: No vertex is visited twice (except possibly first and last)

questions.question: A cycle in a graph is:
options: A simple path where the first and last vertices are the same
A path that visits all vertices once

questions.question: A connected graph is one where:
options: There is a path between every pair of vertices

questions.question: A strongly connected directed graph means:
options: There is a path from every vertex to every other vertex
The graph has no isolated vertices

questions.question: A weakly connected directed graph means:
options: If directions are ignored, the resulting undirected graph is connected

questions.question: In an adjacency matrix representation, G[i][j] = 1 means:
options: There is no edge between i and j
There is an edge between vertex i and vertex j
i and j are the same vertex

questions.question: The adjacency matrix representation has what time complexity to check if a specific edge exists?
A major drawback of the adjacency matrix representation is:
options: It wastes space for sparse graphs

questions.question: In the adjacency list representation, each linked list represents:
options: A row of the adjacency matrix

questions.question: Compared to an adjacency matrix, the adjacency list representation is:
options: More space efficient for sparse graphs

questions.question: In a weighted adjacency list, each entry stores:
options: The neighbor vertex and the edge weight

questions.question: Depth First Search (DFS) uses which data structure internally?
Breadth First Search (BFS) uses which data structure internally?
In DFS, what happens when you reach a dead end (no unvisited neighbors)?
options: You backtrack to the previous vertex
You skip to the next vertex in the list

questions.question: In BFS, after visiting vertex v1, which vertices are visited next?
options: The last vertex in the adjacency list

questions.question: Given graph F-E-D / A-B-C (grid), DFS from A produces which order?
options: A B C F E D
A F E D C B
A B E F C D
A B C D E F

questions.question: Given graph F-E-D / A-B-C (grid), BFS from A produces which order?
options: A B C D E F
A B E D C F
A F E D C B
A B C F E D

questions.question: Topological sorting applies to which type of graph?
In the topological sort algorithm, which vertices are added to the queue first?
options: Vertices added last to the graph

questions.question: In topological sort, after dequeuing vertex w, what happens to its neighbors?
options: They are immediately added to the result
They are removed from the graph

questions.question: Which of the following is a valid application of graphs?
options: Representing web pages connected by hyperlinks

questions.question: A Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) is used for topological sort because:
options: It has no cycles so a valid ordering of dependencies exists
